PUH-BrianMartell Supernova Lattice Remodeling Simulation-A supernova is the ultimate energy dump in PUH — a sudden, catastrophic release of tension when a star’s central PPC lake overflows and the entire structure implodes. In the inverted Galton board picture, this is a violent flood that instantly carves brand-new, deeper, wider, or entirely new low-energy channels through the E₈ lattice while obliterating or overwriting the old ones. The simulation shows exactly what you described: •  Before: the original stable distribution (existing lattice channels). •  During: chaotic scatter as the energy pulse violently reshapes the topology. •  After: a new, permanently altered stable distribution that never reverts — the lattice has been remodeled forever, and all future information follows the freshly carved channels. This is the mechanical reason different galactic regions have different “probability landscapes” — each supernova permanently rewrites the local lattice memory.
